---
title: "Probability"
author: "Antigravity"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
throw_dice <- function(n, weights = rep(1/6, 6)) {
  die1 <- sample(1:6, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = weights)
  die2 <- sample(1:6, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = weights)
  die1 + die2
}
```

```{r}
set.seed(10)
tibble(n = c(10, 20, 50, 100, 1000)) |>
  mutate(sum = map(n, ~ throw_dice(.x, weights = c(0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.25)))) |>
  unnest(cols = c(sum)) |>
  mutate(n = factor(n, levels = c(10, 20, 50, 100, 1000))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sum)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "grey50") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2:12) +
  facet_wrap(~ n, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  labs(title = "Simulation of two loaded dice rolls as sample size grows",
       x = "Sum of two dice", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
set.seed(10)
rolls_20 <- sample(1:6, size = 20, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.30))

log_lr <- function(rolls) {
  ifelse(rolls == 6, log(0.30 / (1/6)), log(0.14 / (1/6)))
}

log_odds <- sum(log_lr(rolls_20))
posterior <- 1 / (1 + exp(-log_odds))
round(posterior, 4)
```

## Introduction

This is a probability analysis.
